intent.operator_problem: The production deployment allows any public visitor to call supabase.auth.signUp(), create an auth user, and enter the onboarding pipeline — creating companies, casinos, and operational environments without approval. This is unacceptable during pilot: tenants are uncontrolled, telemetry is polluted, and the trust boundary between authentication and pilot authorization has collapsed.
intent.pilot_fit: The pilot phase requires controlled access, not growth mechanics. This feature is a minimal defensive gate: it replaces open self-service signup with an admin-approved, allowlist-gated magic-link flow. It is small, does not introduce billing or IAM, and directly reduces operational and reputational risk before broader pilot outreach.
success_outcomes: An unapproved public visitor cannot create an account, receive an OTP, or enter any onboarding route.
An approved email can submit their address, receive a magic link, and proceed through the existing /start route progression.
The allowlist is checked before OTP issuance and again at session time.
Onboarding server actions (register, bootstrap) require a real authenticated session, not a page-level guard only.
A public request-access form exists so prospective operators can apply without reaching any auth surface.
The login UX uses magic-link only; password login is retired from pilot-facing surfaces.
The /auth/sign-up route no longer calls supabase.auth.signUp().
The /start gateway remains the canonical post-auth route resolver.
explicit_exclusions: Seat licensing or trial plan management
Team invites or delegated invite management
Enterprise IAM, SSO, SAML, or OIDC providers
Full admin user-management console or CRM lead pipeline
Custom magic-link infrastructure or bespoke email delivery platform
Multi-channel alert routing for access requests
Invitation expiry workflows beyond the expires_at column
Audit log expansion beyond the existing audit_log_entries table

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Unlinking the signup route would reduce discoverability.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: A reachable route that is not linked is not containment. The route must be replaced, not hidden.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Could reuse existing LoginForm with an allowlist check inserted before authentication.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Password auth introduces forgot-password, update-password, and credential lifecycle surfaces that are unnecessary and create additional exposure for a controlled pilot.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: A single middleware intercept seems clean and DRY.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Supabase session refresh at the edge introduces redirect-loop risk and cookie race conditions. The existing /start gateway pattern is sufficient and already in place.

adjacent_rejected.idea: Full invitation platform with resend, expiry UI, and status dashboard
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Allowlist naturally suggests invite management UX.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: The pilot primitive is binary: email approved? yes/no. A resend/expiry/dashboard platform is out of scope until expansion triggers are met.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: pilot_access_requests could be treated as a sales lead table.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: The table exists for controlled access, not lead management. Enrich with CRM fields only if the expansion trigger rule is met.

adjacent_rejected.idea: Supabase Auth email allowlist at provider level
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Supabase supports domain allowlisting in project settings.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Provider-level allowlist is coarser than needed and bypasses the pilot_access_requests workflow. Application-level check is required for allowlist-before-OTP enforcement.

entities.name: pilot_access_request
entities.description: A pending access request submitted by a prospective pilot operator before any authentication attempt. Not an auth user.
entities.rls_posture: insert: public (anon); select/update: service_role only for pilot

entities.name: approved_email_allowlist
entities.description: A row confirming that a specific email has been manually approved for pilot access. Checked before OTP issuance and again at session time via /start.
entities.rls_posture: insert/update: service_role only; select: authenticated (own email only) and service_role

entities.name: magic_link_login_form
entities.description: Replaces LoginForm. Accepts email only (no password field). On submit, calls the sendMagicLink server action. Displays closed-pilot messaging if the email is not on the allowlist.

entities.name: request_access_form
entities.description: Public form for prospective pilot operators to submit an access request. Creates a pilot_access_request row in pending state. Does not create an auth user.

entities.name: allowlist_gate
entities.description: Server-side check that confirms an email exists in approved_email_allowlist before signInWithOtp() is called. Returns a typed result: approved | not_approved | expired.

capabilities.name: submit_access_request
capabilities.description: Prospective operator submits the public request-access form. Inserts a pending row into pilot_access_requests. No auth user is created.

capabilities.name: check_email_allowlist_before_otp
capabilities.description: Before signInWithOtp() is called, the backend queries approved_email_allowlist for the submitted email. Rejects with closed-pilot messaging if not found. No OTP is sent, no auth user is created or interacted with.

capabilities.name: send_magic_link
capabilities.description: Calls supabase.auth.signInWithOtp() for an approved email. Supabase sends the magic link to the user's inbox. No password is accepted or stored.

capabilities.name: disable_public_signup
capabilities.description: Replaces the /auth/sign-up route and SignUpForm component so supabase.auth.signUp() can no longer be called by any public path. The route redirects to or renders the request-access form.

capabilities.name: validate_allowlist_at_session_time
capabilities.description: After a magic link is verified and a session is established, /start queries approved_email_allowlist for the authenticated user's email before proceeding. A valid session without an allowlist entry is rejected and redirected.

capabilities.name: harden_onboarding_mutations
capabilities.description: Removes skipAuth: true from registerCompanyAction and bootstrapAction. Both server actions must resolve a real authenticated session via withServerAction before executing any mutation. Page-level redirects remain but are not the sole guard.

capabilities.name: retire_password_routes
capabilities.description: Removes or redirects /auth/forgot-password and /auth/update-password. These routes have no function when password login is retired. Redirect target: /auth/login (the new magic-link login).

capabilities.name: display_closed_pilot_messaging
capabilities.description: When an email is not on the allowlist, the login surface renders a clear message and a link to the request-access form. No technical details or auth state are leaked.

capabilities.name: review_access_requests
capabilities.description: Application admin views pending pilot_access_requests through a minimal internal operational moderation surface. The surface lists pending requests with submitter details. It is not a generalized admin dashboard, CRM, or user-management console.

capabilities.name: approve_pilot_access
capabilities.description: Application admin approves a pending access request through the internal review surface. On approval, an entry is inserted into approved_email_allowlist and the request status is set to approved. Operation is server-side and guarded by explicit application-owner/admin authority. Idempotent on retry.

capabilities.name: revoke_pilot_access
capabilities.description: Application admin revokes a previously approved allowlist entry through the internal review surface. Sets the allowlist entry status to revoked. Future OTP issuance and /start progression fail closed for the revoked email. Status-based only — no lifecycle automation.

where.primary_surface: Auth entry surfaces (/auth/login, /signin) and the /start post-auth gateway

surfaces.name: Sign-up route
surfaces.change: replace SignUpForm with RequestAccessForm or redirect to /request-access

surfaces.name: Auth confirm route
surfaces.change: no structural change — already handles OTP token verification

surfaces.name: /start gateway
surfaces.change: add allowlist validation step after auth check, before staff/onboarding routing

surfaces.name: sendMagicLink server action
surfaces.change: new — checks allowlist then calls signInWithOtp()

surfaces.name: registerCompanyAction
surfaces.change: remove skipAuth: true — enforce real session

surfaces.name: bootstrapAction
surfaces.change: remove skipAuth: true — enforce real session

surfaces.name: DB migration — containment tables
surfaces.change: new — creates pilot_access_requests and approved_email_allowlist with RLS

surfaces.name: Internal pilot review page
surfaces.change: new — minimal operational moderation surface: lists pending requests, approve/reject actions

inbound.name: Approved pilot operator submits email on login surface
User clicks magic link in email

outbound.name: Supabase sends magic-link email to approved address
Closed-pilot message displayed for non-approved email
Session established, user routed through /start progression
Unapproved session rejected, redirected to request-access

actors.description: An operator whose email exists in approved_email_allowlist. Can receive a magic link, establish a session, and proceed through the /start → onboarding progression.
permissions: proceed through onboarding if allowlist valid at session time
restrictions: cannot create an account without prior admin approval
session-time allowlist check must pass at /start

actors.description: Any person who reaches the production domain without prior approval. Can only submit a request-access form.
permissions: view closed-pilot messaging on login surface
restrictions: cannot create a company, casino, or operational environment

actors.description: A user who has an existing Supabase auth session but whose email is not in approved_email_allowlist (e.g., stale session, revoked approval). Session is rejected at /start.
permissions: arrive at /start with a valid session
restrictions: cannot pass the session-time allowlist check
redirected to request-access with appropriate messaging

actors.description: The builder/owner who manually reviews pilot_access_requests and inserts rows into approved_email_allowlist. No automated approval path exists for this slice.
permissions: read pilot_access_requests (via Supabase dashboard or direct DB access)
update pilot_access_requests status to approved or rejected
restrictions: no automated approval workflow in this slice
pilot access review is performed through a minimal internal operational moderation surface. The surface is intentionally constrained and is not a generalized administrative platform.

trigger_events.name: magic_link_clicked
preconditions: token_hash and type params present in URL at /auth/confirm

trigger_events.name: session_arrives_at_start
preconditions: user has an authenticated Supabase session

trigger_events.name: onboarding_mutation_invoked
preconditions: user is in onboarding route (/register or /bootstrap)

timing_constraints: Allowlist check occurs server-side before any call to supabase.auth.signInWithOtp().
Session-time allowlist check occurs inside /start, before any staff or casino routing.
Admin approval is manual for this slice; no SLA or timeout is enforced.
expires_at on allowlist entries is optional and not enforced by application logic in this slice — database constraint only.

business_rules.statement: No OTP may be issued to an email that does not exist in approved_email_allowlist at the time of the login attempt.
supabase.auth.signUp() must not be callable by any public-facing route or component.
Authentication alone does not imply pilot authorization. A valid session must also have an active allowlist entry to proceed past /start.
Onboarding server actions (registerCompanyAction, bootstrapAction) must resolve a real authenticated session. skipAuth: true must be removed or neutralized.
Password-based login (signInWithPassword) must not appear in any pilot-facing UX surface.
The /start gateway remains the canonical post-auth route resolver. No new orchestration layer or middleware intercept replaces it.
A failed allowlist check must not reveal technical details. The user-facing message must reference the request-access path only.
pilot_access_requests inserts are publicly writable (anon role). No auth is required to submit a request. The table must not expose other rows to public read.

The internal review surface is limited to operational moderation of pilot access requests and must not evolve into a generalized admin platform, IAM system, CRM, or user-management console. Any expansion beyond approve/reject/revoke and pending-request listing requires a separate FIB.

invariants: If authentication alone can create an operator environment, the pilot boundary is broken.
The allowlist check is authoritative at both pre-OTP and session-time checkpoints — neither check alone is sufficient.
onboarding mutations are guarded at the action layer, not only at the page/route layer.
decision_notes: Magic-link OTP is the sole login method for pilot. No password path survives this slice.
Admin approval is out-of-band (Supabase dashboard) for Phase 1. A minimal in-app admin surface is deferred to Phase 3.
The /auth/confirm route requires no structural change — it already handles verifyOtp() token processing.
FIB-H §H3 explicitly rejects global middleware — /start extension is the approved containment point.
expires_at is stored but not enforced by application logic in this slice. Enforcement can be added if the expansion trigger rule is met.
Two login surfaces exist (/auth/login and /signin under marketing layout). Both must be updated to MagicLinkForm.

loop.action: attempts to reach /auth/sign-up or any password-login path
loop.system_response: redirects to /request-access; supabase.auth.signUp() and signInWithPassword() are not reachable from any public surface

loop.action: fills in the request-access form with name, email, casino name, role, table count, and optional message
loop.system_response: form is publicly accessible with no auth requirement

loop.action: requestPilotAccess server action inserts a pending row into pilot_access_requests
loop.system_response: returns success; no auth user is created; user sees confirmation messaging

loop.action: reviews pending pilot_access_requests in Supabase dashboard; approves eligible requests
loop.system_response: admin inserts approved email into approved_email_allowlist and sets request status to approved

loop.action: navigates to /auth/login or /signin and submits their approved email address
loop.system_response: sendMagicLink server action is called; backend queries approved_email_allowlist

loop.action: allowlist check fails (email not found or expired)
loop.system_response: no OTP is sent; no auth user is created or looked up; user receives closed-pilot message with link to /request-access
supabase.auth.signInWithOtp() is called; Supabase sends magic link to the approved email

loop.action: clicks the magic link from their inbox
loop.system_response: /auth/confirm route calls supabase.auth.verifyOtp(); on success, redirects to /start

loop.action: /start gateway checks authentication and then queries approved_email_allowlist for the session user's email
loop.system_response: if allowlist check fails (stale session, revoked entry), user is redirected to request-access with messaging
existing /start routing logic proceeds: staff binding check → registration check → casino setup check → /pit

loop.action: proceeds through /register or /bootstrap onboarding steps and submits a server action
loop.system_response: server action (registerCompanyAction or bootstrapAction) resolves the authenticated session via withServerAction; no skipAuth bypass; mutation proceeds only with a verified session

required_existing: Supabase Auth project configured with OTP/magic-link email enabled
Existing /auth/confirm route handling verifyOtp() — already in place
Existing /start gateway as canonical post-auth router — already in place
withServerAction compositor used by onboarding server actions — already in place
Supabase email delivery operational (Supabase built-in SMTP or custom SMTP configured)
missing_dependencies: sendMagicLink server action — does not exist
requestPilotAccess server action — does not exist
Allowlist query in /start gateway — not yet added
skipAuth removal from registerCompanyAction and bootstrapAction — not yet done
assumptions: Supabase OTP email delivery works without custom SMTP for pilot scale.
The application admin can manage allowlist entries directly in the Supabase dashboard for Phase 1.
The /start gateway pattern is stable enough to extend without restructuring.
withServerAction skipping session enforcement via skipAuth: true was a temporary scaffold, not an intentional architectural pattern.
The LoginForm password path is not used by any internal tooling, integration test fixture, or E2E test that must remain in production mode.

outcomes.statement: An unapproved public visitor cannot create an account, receive an OTP, or enter any onboarding route.
An approved email can submit their address, receive a magic link, and proceed through the existing /start route progression.
A public request-access path exists for prospective operators who are not yet approved.
The allowlist is validated at session time; a stale or revoked approval cannot grant ongoing access.
Failed allowlist checks display closed-pilot messaging with a request-access path. No technical details are leaked.
Onboarding server actions require a real authenticated session. Page-level guards are no longer the sole protection.
The /start gateway remains the canonical post-auth router. No global middleware layer is introduced.
Internal pilot review can list pending access requests, approve, reject, and revoke pilot access through a minimal operational moderation surface. The surface does not exceed this scope.

open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold: Exact Supabase SMTP configuration for magic-link delivery
Whether /auth/sign-up is redirected to /request-access or replaced in place
Exact error message copy for closed-pilot messaging
Whether expires_at enforcement is activated in this slice or deferred
Whether the /auth/update-password and /auth/forgot-password routes are hard-deleted or soft-redirected
expansion_triggers: More than one admin must manage pilot approvals
Multiple pilot properties require delegated invite control
Pilot access must support teams or seats
External customers require formal account administration
Regulatory or security review requires deeper audit trails
